preamble: Similar to its voiceless counterpart, the voiced palatalized velar stop [ɡʲ] shifts its place of articulation forward to the postalveolar region, acquiring a fricative release to become the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ].

examples.note: Velar /g/ shifted to /dʒ/ (later /ʒ/ in many Slavic languages) before front vowels.
Latin /g/ before front vowels /e, i/ regularly developed into [dʒ] in Italian.
Old English palatalization of geminate velars or velars in specific environments yielded [dʒ].

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
